commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: I know: I see, that is, that thou art diligent in the practice of good works, and bearest calmly the insufferable injuries of the wicked, for thou hast diligently examined the words and works of the false Apostles, and hast not been willing to give way to them in anything. <a

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: All these things tend to praise, and that no small praise; and it behooves such men, and such a class, and such elected persons, by all means to be admonished, that they may not be defrauded of such privileges granted to them of God. These few things He said that He had against them.

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: In the first epistle He speaks thus: I know that you suffer and work, I see that you are patient; think not that I am staying long from you.
